concept_decisions.labels_compared: ensemble_forecasting vs machine_learning_forecasting vs mechanistic_statistical_hybrids vs time_series_forecasting vs forecasting_dynamics_models
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: Slice manifest; source concept table rows for all conflicting labels; allowed concepts table; titles and complete available abstracts for all 150 slice records.
concept_decisions.rationale: Multi-model forecast aggregation is a distinct forecasting strategy and is not interchangeable with single-model statistical forecasting, machine-learning forecasting, hybrid mechanistic-statistical forecasting, or generic dynamics-model papers.

concept_decisions.labels_compared: forecast_calibration_evaluation vs ensemble_forecasting vs machine_learning_forecasting vs time_series_forecasting
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: Slice manifest; source concept table rows for all conflicting labels; allowed concepts table; titles and complete available abstracts for all 150 slice records.
concept_decisions.rationale: Calibration, scoring, and forecast-evaluation work targets predictive performance assessment rather than forecast-generation machinery, so it should remain separate from ensemble, machine-learning, and time-series forecast production concepts.

concept_decisions.labels_compared: machine_learning_forecasting vs time_series_forecasting vs mechanistic_statistical_hybrids vs ensemble_forecasting
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: Slice manifest; source concept table rows for all conflicting labels; allowed concepts table; titles and complete available abstracts for all 150 slice records.
concept_decisions.rationale: Machine-learning forecasting implies a distinct predictive paradigm from classical time-series methods, forecast ensembles, and mechanistic-statistical hybrids, so it should not be collapsed into those neighboring concepts.

concept_decisions.labels_compared: mechanistic_statistical_hybrids vs machine_learning_forecasting vs time_series_forecasting vs forecasting_dynamics_models
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: Slice manifest; source concept table rows for all conflicting labels; allowed concepts table; titles and complete available abstracts for all 150 slice records.
concept_decisions.rationale: Hybrid models combine mechanistic transmission structure with statistical observation or forecasting layers and are semantically distinct from pure statistical forecasting, pure mechanistic simulation, and generic residual dynamics-model buckets.

concept_decisions.retained_canonical_name: Other infectious-disease forecasting and dynamics models
concept_decisions.labels_compared: forecasting_dynamics_models vs surveillance_early_warning_systems vs predictive_early_warning_models vs syndromic_surveillance
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: Slice manifest; source concept table rows for all conflicting labels; allowed concepts table; titles and complete available abstracts for all 150 slice records.
concept_decisions.rationale: The slice shows this label functioning as a residual catchall that mixes early-warning system design, syndromic surveillance implementations, predictive early-warning tools, and at least one genuinely residual modeling-framework paper; it should therefore be split while preserving a narrow residual bucket.

concept_decisions.labels_compared: outbreak_anomaly_detection vs outbreak_detection_algorithms vs algorithmic_outbreak_detection_and_early_warning vs surveillance_early_warning_systems vs syndromic_surveillance vs genomic_outbreak_detection
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: Slice manifest; source concept table rows for all conflicting labels; allowed concepts table; titles and complete available abstracts for all 150 slice records.
concept_decisions.rationale: Records under this label span statistical detection algorithms, operational early-warning systems, program evaluations, genomic cluster detection, syndromic surveillance, measurement refinements, and simulation-based benchmarking; those are distinct method families and should not remain collapsed in one umbrella concept.

concept_decisions.labels_compared: renewal_reproduction_number_models vs reproduction_number_estimation_and_monitoring vs reproduction_number_methods_theory vs reproduction_number_threshold_theory vs transmission_dynamics_inference vs reproduction_number_control_planning_models
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: Slice manifest; source concept table rows for all conflicting labels; allowed concepts table; titles and complete available abstracts for all 150 slice records.
concept_decisions.rationale: The slice mixes true renewal-based estimation papers with broader empirical R estimation, theoretical threshold work, control-planning models, transmission-tree inference, reviews, and mechanistic simulations; those preserve different estimands and identification assumptions and must be separated.

concept_decisions.labels_compared: time_series_forecasting vs machine_learning_forecasting vs ensemble_forecasting vs mechanistic_statistical_hybrids
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: Slice manifest; source concept table rows for all conflicting labels; allowed concepts table; titles and complete available abstracts for all 150 slice records.
concept_decisions.rationale: Classical statistical time-series forecasting remains a distinct forecast-generation concept and should not be merged with ensemble, machine-learning, hybrid, or residual dynamics-model concepts.

concerns: No slice-assigned records directly instantiated ensemble_forecasting, forecast_calibration_evaluation, machine_learning_forecasting, mechanistic_statistical_hybrids, or time_series_forecasting; those retain decisions rely on label semantics plus source-table context.

Several boundaries remain close by design, especially outbreak-detection algorithms versus operational early-warning systems and applied versus empirical reproduction-number studies, but the slice abstracts were sufficient to separate them consistently here.
